framework.name: NCSC Password Administration Guidance
framework.scope: Guidance for system administrators on implementing password policies that balance security with usability, including the "three random words" approach
framework.structure_description: Controls covering password policies, storage, multi-factor authentication, and organisational password management

controls.title: Reduce reliance on passwords
controls.description: Implement technical controls that reduce reliance on user passwords as the primary authentication mechanism. Use multi-factor authentication, single sign-on, and hardware tokens to reduce the number of passwords users must manage.
controls.description_nl: Implement technical controls that reduce reliance on user passwords as the primary authentication mechanism. Use multi-factor authentication, single sign-on, and hardware tokens to reduce the number of passwords users must manage.
controls.implementation_guidance: Deploy MFA across all systems. Implement SSO to reduce password count. Consider passwordless authentication (FIDO2, Windows Hello) for suitable systems.
controls.verification_guidance: Review MFA deployment coverage. Count passwords per user. Verify SSO integration.

controls.title: Implement multi-factor authentication
controls.description: Deploy MFA on all internet-facing services and privileged access. MFA significantly reduces the risk of password compromise leading to account takeover. Prefer authenticator apps or hardware tokens over SMS.
controls.description_nl: Deploy MFA on all internet-facing services and privileged access. MFA significantly reduces the risk of password compromise leading to account takeover. Prefer authenticator apps or hardware tokens over SMS.
controls.implementation_guidance: Enable MFA for all internet-accessible services. Prioritise privileged accounts. Use authenticator apps (TOTP) or FIDO2 hardware tokens. Avoid SMS-based MFA where possible.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify MFA is enforced. Check MFA method distribution (app vs SMS vs hardware). Test MFA bypass resistance.

controls.title: Use the "three random words" approach for user passwords
controls.title_nl: Use the "three random words" approach for user passwords
controls.description: NCSC recommends using three random words combined as a passphrase rather than enforcing complex password rules (uppercase, numbers, symbols). Three random words create passwords that are long enough to be secure but memorable enough that users do not need to write them down.
controls.description_nl: NCSC recommends using three random words combined as a passphrase rather than enforcing complex password rules (uppercase, numbers, symbols). Three random words create passwords that are long enough to be secure but memorable enough that users do not need to write them down.
controls.implementation_guidance: Communicate the three random words approach to users. Set minimum password length to 12 characters (typical three-word passphrase). Remove complexity requirements (mandatory uppercase, numbers, symbols) that lead to predictable patterns.
controls.verification_guidance: Review password policy for length vs complexity requirements. Verify user guidance promotes three random words.

controls.title: Do not enforce regular password expiry
controls.title_nl: Do not enforce regular password expiry
controls.description: NCSC recommends against enforcing regular password changes (e.g., every 90 days). Forced expiry leads to predictable password patterns (Password1, Password2, etc.) and does not improve security. Passwords should only be changed when there is evidence of compromise.
controls.description_nl: NCSC recommends against enforcing regular password changes (e.g., every 90 days). Forced expiry leads to predictable password patterns (Password1, Password2, etc.) and does not improve security. Passwords should only be changed when there is evidence of compromise.
controls.implementation_guidance: Remove mandatory password expiry policies. Implement breach detection to trigger password resets when needed. Use credential monitoring services to detect compromised passwords.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify no mandatory password expiry is configured. Check that breach-triggered reset process exists.

controls.title: Block common and breached passwords
controls.description: Implement a deny list of common and previously breached passwords. Check new passwords against known-compromised password databases (such as Have I Been Pwned) at the point of creation or change.
controls.description_nl: Implement a deny list of common and previously breached passwords. Check new passwords against known-compromised password databases (such as Have I Been Pwned) at the point of creation or change.
controls.implementation_guidance: Integrate with a breached password database (e.g., Have I Been Pwned Passwords API). Block the most common passwords. Prevent passwords containing the username, organisation name, or other easily guessed terms.
controls.verification_guidance: Test password creation with known-common passwords. Verify they are rejected. Check breached password database integration.

controls.title: Protect password storage
controls.description: Store passwords securely using modern hashing algorithms with salting. Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2id. Never store passwords in plaintext or with reversible encryption. Protect password hashes from unauthorised access.
controls.description_nl: Store passwords securely using modern hashing algorithms with salting. Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2id. Never store passwords in plaintext or with reversible encryption. Protect password hashes from unauthorised access.
controls.implementation_guidance: Use Argon2id (preferred), bcrypt, or scrypt for password hashing. Apply unique salts per password. Protect password databases with strong access controls. Monitor for unauthorised access to password stores.
controls.verification_guidance: Audit password storage implementation. Verify modern hashing is used. Check access controls on password databases.

controls.title: Help users manage passwords securely
controls.description: Provide users with tools and guidance to manage passwords securely. Allow and encourage the use of password managers. Provide clear guidance on creating strong passwords and recognising phishing attempts.
controls.description_nl: Provide users with tools and guidance to manage passwords securely. Allow and encourage the use of password managers. Provide clear guidance on creating strong passwords and recognising phishing attempts.
controls.implementation_guidance: Deploy an enterprise password manager. Allow browser-based password saving for non-privileged accounts. Provide user guidance on the three random words approach and when to use a password manager.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify password manager deployment. Review user guidance materials. Check password manager adoption rates.

controls.title: Monitor for compromised credentials
controls.description: Actively monitor for compromised credentials through breach notification services, dark web monitoring, and credential stuffing detection. When compromised credentials are detected, force password resets and investigate potential impact.
controls.description_nl: Actively monitor for compromised credentials through breach notification services, dark web monitoring, and credential stuffing detection. When compromised credentials are detected, force password resets and investigate potential impact.
controls.implementation_guidance: Subscribe to breach notification services. Implement credential stuffing detection. Monitor authentication logs for anomalous patterns. Automate password reset for confirmed compromised accounts.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify breach monitoring is active. Review credential compromise response process. Check detection capabilities.
